section_name: Suspension, Revocation, Cancellation, or Denial of Driver’s License Under Other Laws
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 522. Commercial Driver's Licenses
##### § 522.092 — Suspension, Revocation, Cancellation, or Denial of Driver’s License Under Other Laws

A person subject to disqualification under this chapter may also have the person’s driver’s license suspended, revoked, canceled, or denied under one or more of the following, if the conduct that is a ground for disqualification is also a ground for the suspension, revocation, cancellation, or denial of a driver’s license suspension under:

    (1) Chapter 521 (Driver’s Licenses and Certificates) ;

    (2) Chapter 524 (Administrative Suspension of Driver’s License for Failure to Pass Test for Intoxication) ;

    (3) Chapter 601 (Motor Vehicle Safety Responsibility Act) ; or

    (4) Chapter 724 (Implied Consent) .

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: A person subject to disqualification under this chapter may also have the person’s driver’s license suspended, revoked, canceled, or denied under one or more of the following, if the conduct that is a ground for disqualification is also a ground for the suspension, revocation, cancellation, or denial of a driver’s license suspension under:
Chapter 524 (Administrative Suspension of Driver’s License for Failure to Pass Test for Intoxication) ;
Chapter 601 (Motor Vehicle Safety Responsibility Act) ; or
